title: Six Owlets
content:
Six owlets sitting in a tree,
        six cats in effigy,
        six of both in a boat
        the leeward lives in Innisfree.

        Six women marching
        through a park,
        six lanterns at rest
        six cauldrons to
        six walking abreast.

        In the still of the morning
        I'd hazard a guess
        there's a little less.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Paul Cameron Brown, titled "Six Owlets", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
